items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.Network` **Service directory:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/` **Triggered by:** commit 2404cbdff (PR #58200) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed The 1.1.0 stable release of `Azure.Provisioning.Network` added significant new functionality: - Expanded from 37 to 120 Network resource types - Added polymorphic type support for `BaseAdminRule` (`NetworkAdminRule`, `NetworkDefaultAdminRule`), `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`, and `FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes - Added support for 6 new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources ## Gaps Found - **Empty `Authenticate the Client` section** — The heading exists in the README but has no content at all - **Empty `Next steps` section** — The heading exists but contains no links or guidance - **No examples for `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources** — The 6 new NSP resource types (`NetworkSecurityPerimeter`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAssociation`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLink`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLoggingConfiguration`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile`) added in 1.1.0 are undocumented in the README - **No examples for polymorphic types** — The new `BaseAdminRule`/`NetworkAdminRule`/`NetworkDefaultAdminRule` hierarchy and `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`/`FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes have no README coverage despite being prominent 1.1.0 features - **Thin `Key concepts` section** — With the library now covering 120 resource types, the key concepts section should enumerate major resource categories and note the polymorphic type pattern
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` — **Edit** 1. Fill in the `Authenticate the Client` section (currently empty after the header). Add a note that this is a provisioning/infrastructure library using `DefaultAzureCredential` via `azd` and does not require client-side authentication at resource definition time, e.g.: ```markdown ### Authenticate the client This library is used for defining infrastructure declaratively in .NET and generating Bicep templates for deployment via [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Authentication happens at deployment time through `azd`; no client credential is required when defining resources in code. ``` 2. Expand the `Key concepts` section to mention the resource count and polymorphic type pattern: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. `Azure.Provisioning.Network` provides over 120 Network resource types including virtual networks, subnets, load balancers, firewalls, NAT gateways, network security groups, network watchers, and more. **Polymorphic types:** Some resources support polymorphic hierarchies. For example, `BaseAdminRule` is the base type with `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as concrete subtypes. Similarly, `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo` and `FirewallPolicyRule` follow this pattern. ``` 3. Add a new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` example in the `Examples` section. 4. Add a `Next steps` content entry: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Provisioning libraries for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - Use [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure - Browse additional [Azure.Provisioning.*]((www.nuget.org/redacted) packages for other Azure services ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Write sample code in a new or existing test file. The existing file to add regions to is: `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/tests/BasicNetworkTests.cs` Add a new test method with a `#region Snippet:NetworkSecurityPerimeterCreate` region that demonstrates creating a `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` with at least one child resource (e.g., `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile` or `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`). Wrap any test-only setup/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. Add a new test method with a `#region Snippet:BaseAdminRulePolymorphic` region demonstrating defining a `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as subtypes of `BaseAdminRule`. Then add the corresponding README snippet placeholders in the Examples section: ```markdown ### Create a Network Security Perimeter ```C# Snippet:NetworkSecurityPerimeterCreate ``` ``` ```markdown ### Use polymorphic admin rules ```C# Snippet:BaseAdminRulePolymorphic ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` currently has all required sections but two are empty: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ✅ | | Getting started > Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Too brief for v1.1.0 scope | | Examples | ⚠️ Missing 1.1.0 feature examples | | Troubleshooting | ✅ | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | | Contributing | ✅ | ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 network` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from test files and injects the code into README.md placeholder blocks. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 network` — regenerates public API listing files. Required if any public types or members were added or changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Network] Docs: Azure.Provisioning.Network README missing 1.1.0 feature examples and incomplete sections
